skill.name: amazon-product-search-api-skill
skill.description: Automates Amazon product search data extraction using the BrowserAct API. Returns structured product data including prices, ratings, review counts, monthly sales estimates, Best Seller tags, and delivery information without requiring captcha handling or IP management.
skill.summary: Extract structured Amazon product search results via the BrowserAct API — no captchas, no IP restrictions, no AI hallucinations.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: All 75 static analyzer findings (risk score 100/100) were evaluated and determined to be false positives. Network calls target only the documented BrowserAct API endpoint (api.browseract.com). Environment variable access is limited to BROWSERACT_API_KEY, a single documented credential. External command detections are all markdown backtick formatting in SKILL.md, not actual shell execution. The critical heuristic flagging code execution + network + credential access describes a legitimate API client pattern: the bundled Python script reads its API key and authenticates with its own service endpoint. No data exfiltration, no obfuscation, no malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Key
low_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from the environment to authenticate with the BrowserAct API. This is a legitimate and documented requirement, but any skill that accesses environment variables for credentials carries inherent risk if the execution environment is compromised.

low_findings.title: Network Requests to External API
low_findings.description: The script makes HTTPS requests to api.browseract.com for task creation, status polling, and result retrieval. All requests use Bearer token authentication and target a single documented API service. No requests are made to unexpected or untrusted domains.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Execution via Agent Instructions
low_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs the AI agent to execute a Python script via shell command. The script is bundled with the skill and its arguments are passed as JSON payload values to the API rather than interpolated into shell strings. The command template in the documentation shows properly quoted arguments.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Shell Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged 43 occurrences of backtick characters in SKILL.md as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All flagged instances are standard markdown formatting: inline code spans using single backticks and fenced code blocks using triple backticks. No actual Ruby/shell backtick execution exists in this skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Keyword Text Misidentified as Weak Cryptography
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged the word 'keywords' and parameter name 'KeyWords' in SKILL.md as weak cryptographic algorithm references. These terms refer to Amazon search keywords, not cryptographic keys. No cryptographic operations exist anywhere in this skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded API Base URL
dangerous_patterns.description: The script hardcodes the BrowserAct API base URL (api.browseract.com/v2/workflow) and the console URL (browseract.com/reception/integrations). These are the documented service endpoints for the BrowserAct platform and are necessary for the skill to function. No requests are made to other domains.

content.user_title: Search and Extract Amazon Product Data via BrowserAct API
content.value_statement: Manually collecting structured product data from Amazon is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates Amazon product search data extraction through the BrowserAct API, delivering structured results without captchas or IP restrictions.
actual_capabilities: Search Amazon for products by keywords with brand filters applied
Extract structured product data including prices, ratings, and review counts
Support multi-language Amazon search across 8 languages
Return up to 100 products per search query from paginated results
Identify Best Seller tags and product availability status
Retrieve estimated monthly sales data for market analysis
limitations: Requires a BrowserAct API key to authenticate with the service
Task execution can take several minutes due to automated browser operations
Data accuracy depends on Amazon current page structure and availability
Monthly sales estimates are approximations and may not be available for all products

use_cases.title: Market Researcher
use_cases.description: Extract product listings, prices, and ratings for competitive analysis across brands and categories on Amazon.

use_cases.title: E-commerce Seller
use_cases.description: Monitor competitor pricing, track Best Seller badges, and analyze shipping options for your product category.

use_cases.title: Data Analyst
use_cases.description: Build structured datasets of Amazon product data including monthly sales estimates for trend analysis and reporting.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Product Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Amazon for wireless earbuds from Sony and give me the top 20 results with prices and ratings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: Simple keyword and brand search

prompt_templates.title: Best Seller Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all Apple products on Amazon with Best Seller tags, limit to 50 items, and show me prices, ratings, and review counts.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Filtered search with specific attributes

prompt_templates.title: Localized Multi-field Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Amazon Japan for Samsung tablets, extract product URLs, ratings, review counts, and monthly sales estimates for the top 100 results.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Language-specific search with full data extraction

prompt_templates.title: Multi-brand Competitive Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a competitive analysis: search for laptop stands across multiple brands (Rain Design, Twelve South, OMOTON), comparing prices, ratings, delivery info, and Best Seller status for each brand with 50 results per brand.
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert: Cross-brand comparison with parallel searches

output_examples.output: Found 25 Apple AirPods products on Amazon. Top results include Apple AirPods Pro (2nd Gen) at $199.99 with 4.7 stars from 15,000 reviews, and Apple AirPods (3rd Gen) at $149.99 with 4.6 stars from 12,000 reviews. Both products are currently available with free delivery.

Scanned 50 Samsung phone listings on Amazon. Found 3 Best Seller tagged products: Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra at $1,299.99 (4.5 stars, 8,200 reviews), Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 6 at $1,799.99 (4.3 stars, 4,500 reviews), and Samsung Galaxy A55 at $399.99 (4.4 stars, 11,000 reviews). All three are currently available for purchase.

best_practices: Set the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable before running the skill to avoid authentication failures
Use specific brand names together with targeted keywords for more precise search results
Monitor the script timestamped status output to track progress of long-running tasks
anti_patterns: Do not run multiple searches simultaneously with the same API key as this may trigger rate limiting
Do not retry more than once on authorization errors since they indicate an invalid or expired API key
Do not expect instant results as automated Amazon page browsing takes time to complete

faq.question: Do I need an Amazon account to use this skill?
faq.answer: No, this skill uses the BrowserAct API which handles Amazon access automatically without requiring your own Amazon account.

faq.question: What is BrowserAct and why do I need an API key?
faq.answer: BrowserAct is a browser automation service that provides pre-built workflows for web data extraction. You can get a free API key from the BrowserAct website to use their Amazon search templates.

faq.question: How long does a typical search take to complete?
faq.answer: Most searches complete within 2 to 3 minutes, though larger result sets of 100 products may take up to 5 minutes due to pagination.

faq.question: Can I search Amazon in different countries and languages?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports 8 language settings including English, German, French, Italian, Spanish, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and Traditional Chinese.

faq.question: What product data fields are returned in the results?
faq.answer: Results include product title, product URL, rating score, review count, current price, list price, delivery information, shipping location, Best Seller status, monthly sales estimates, and availability.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many products I can extract per search?
faq.answer: You can request up to 100 products per search. The skill handles pagination automatically across multiple pages of Amazon search results.
